name: Synthesis Overreach appraisal for Tad Mood Wellbeing Quality Life bundle
summary: Accepted. The claim synthesis stays within the support map: it reports insufficient direct healthy-user evidence, preserves indirect ED/BPH/CSVD context, treats positive findings as symptom-mediated or uncontrolled, and uses ETLAS-2 plus safety findings as boundaries. It does not recommend off-label use or present tadalafil as a general mood, vitality, sleep, or well-being agent.

findings.claim_or_issue: The public claim posture is insufficient direct evidence for healthy men, not evidence of efficacy or proof of no effect.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the correct synthesis for a baseline built from indirect ED/BPH/CSVD evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The synthesis repeatedly distinguishes ED/BPH symptom-mediated quality-of-life effects from direct mood or broad well-being benefit.
findings.why_it_matters: The most tempting overreach in this topic is converting sexual or urinary symptom relief into a general wellness claim.
findings.recommended_action: No correction required; preserve this boundary in public summaries.
